# Hologram OS — Documentation

> Developer and AI-agent documentation for Hologram OS: a sovereign, serverless internet computer that boots in the browser. Every object is self-verifying — its identity is the hash of its content, re-derived on receipt (Law L5). These docs are static, content-addressed, and generated in part from the source so they cannot silently drift.

## Get started
- [What is Hologram OS?](/docs): the 60-second mental model.
- [Quickstart](/docs/quickstart): boot it, build on it, or connect an agent.

## Concepts (why)
- [The substrate](/docs/concepts/substrate): κ, self-verifying bytes, sealing, the closure.
- [The five Laws](/docs/concepts/the-five-laws): the constraints every object obeys.
- [The boot chain](/docs/concepts/boot-chain): index → splash → login → shell.
- [Proof, not promises](/docs/concepts/conformance): witnesses, the gate, conformance.

## Build (how)
- [Build, run, share an app](/docs/build/an-app): the three verbs end to end.

## Reference (what)
- [The κ-route](/docs/reference/kappa-route): resolve any object by its hash over HTTP.
- [The Q door](/docs/reference/q): recall · fuse · factory · hermes, with signatures.
- [App manifest](/docs/reference/app-manifest): the holospace.json shape.
- [Conformance rows](/docs/reference/conformance): 112 proven claims (generated).
- [Agent doors](/docs/reference/agent-doors): MCP, NANDA, A2A, the .well-known map.

## Architecture
- [Architecture decisions](/docs/architecture/adrs): 91 ADRs, the primary sources (generated).

## Machine-readable
- [conformance.json](/docs/reference/conformance.json): the gate's rows as data.
- [adrs.json](/docs/reference/adrs.json): every ADR as data.
- The OS-level agent map lives at [/.well-known/agents.json](/.well-known/agents.json).
